title: Deepen live progress implementation
goal: Refactor live progress and shell command monitoring into a small internal interface or helper so long-running run observability stays product-quality while preserving behavior.
acceptance: Live progress update logic is centralized behind a small internal interface or helper so workflow phases do not manually duplicate low-level state/event persistence details.
Shell command output streaming remains bounded, cancellation-safe, and preserves stdout/stderr for command results.
khazad-doom status --run still includes the progress snapshot and khazad-doom watch --run still renders compact human-readable progress.
No hidden global workflow timeout is introduced; verification/gate command timeouts remain explicit per-command guardrails.
Existing tests pass, and at least one integration or unit test continues to cover live progress during long verification.
must_ask_if: The refactor requires changing public CLI flags, JSON status shape, workflow slice schema, or timeout semantics.
